programs.name: ARKansas Medicaid Long-Term Services and Supports (LTSS)
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical coverage for low-income seniors and disabled individuals, including long-term care services. In Arkansas, it covers nursing home care and home-based alternatives through waivers.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ and disabled individuals meeting income and asset limits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by eligibility; covers eligible medical and long-term care costs.

programs.name: ARChoices in Homecare
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services waiver to help seniors avoid nursing homes. Provides personal care, adult day services, and homemaker assistance.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled adults needing nursing home level care but preferring home living, meeting financial criteria.
programs.benefit_value: Up to authorized hours of services per month based on care plan.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings. Includes adult day health, transportation, and home care as an alternative to institutional care.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified for nursing home level care who can live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive capitated coverage for eligible services.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for eligible low-income seniors. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and costs; SLMB covers Part B; QI offers partial Part B help.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+ meeting specific income/asset thresholds.
programs.benefit_value: Full or partial coverage of Medicare costs depending on program (e.g., QMB up to $1,255 individual income limit monthly).

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing food at approved retailers. Helps low-income households afford nutritious meals.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ with limited resources.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200+ per month per eligible household, scaled by income.

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Assists with heating and cooling bills, crisis aid, and weatherproofing for low-income homes. Administered through local agencies statewide.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors meeting federal poverty guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants averaging $300-$500 for energy bills.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Improves energy efficiency in low-income homes through insulation, sealing, and furnace repairs. Reduces utility costs and health risks from poor conditions.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and disabled households prioritized.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements valued at $5,000+ per household.

programs.name: Senior Medicare Patrol (SMP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free Medicare counseling to detect fraud, waste, and abuse. Provides education and assistance navigating Medicare benefits.[1]
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries aged 65+, especially those at risk of scams.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling and training sessions.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors through Area Agencies on Aging. Promotes nutrition and reduces isolation with daily check-ins.[4][5]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound low-income seniors aged 60+ unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: 1-2 free meals per day, 5 days a week.

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, training, and support to family caregivers of seniors. Offers counseling and supplemental services to prevent caregiver burnout.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors aged 60+ or grandparents raising children.
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $1,600 annually plus training.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid community service jobs with training for older workers. Helps transition to unsubsidized employment.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage work up to 20 hours/week.

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors (via Area Agencies on Aging)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal assistance for issues like benefits, housing, and abuse. Connects seniors to pro bono services through local AAAs.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ facing legal barriers.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes and assisted living. Investigates complaints and ensures quality care rights.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors in long-term care facilities and their families.

programs.name: Arkansas Property Tax Relief for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides a credit against property taxes for eligible seniors to reduce housing costs. The program offers relief based on income and disability status, helping seniors remain in their homes.
programs.who_its_for: Arkansas residents aged 65+ or totally and permanently disabled, with household income under $50,000 (2023 limits).
programs.whats_unique: State-specific tax credit unique to Arkansas, not available in all states; supplements federal programs by directly reducing local property taxes.
programs.benefit_value: Up to $400 credit per qualifying homestead, depending on income bracket.

programs.name: Arkansas PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: Coordinates comprehensive primary, preventive, acute, and long-term care services at PACE centers, in homes, and inpatient facilities. A multidisciplinary team develops personalized care plans covering Medicare/Medicaid services plus additional needs.
programs.who_its_for: Individuals aged 55+ requiring nursing facility level of care but able to live safely in the community in served Arkansas counties.
programs.whats_unique: State-implemented PACE program with specific centers in Northwest, Northeast, and Western Arkansas; provides extras beyond standard federal Medicare/Medicaid not universally available.
programs.benefit_value: All approved Medicare/Medicaid services at no additional cost to dual-eligible participants; additional services as determined (e.g., transportation, home care).

programs.name: Family Caregiver Grant Program (Alzheimer’s Arkansas via CareLink)
programs.what_it_does: Awards grants to caregivers for respite services, allowing time for personal needs. Administered to support those caring for seniors with chronic illnesses.
programs.who_its_for: Caregivers of individuals 60+ with chronic illness living in Central Arkansas (Pulaski County).
programs.whats_unique: Arkansas-specific grant program focused on respite for caregivers in designated service areas, beyond standard federal caregiver support.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by grant award; funds respite services (amounts not fixed, based on application).
